facts: Pluto has a thin atmosphere, primarily nitrogen, like Earth's.
Its highly elliptical orbit takes it from 4.4 billion km to 7.4 billion km from the Sun.
At its farthest point, temperatures plummet below -240°C, freezing the nitrogen solid onto the surface.
idea.payoff: For decades, Pluto's entire atmosphere effectively collapses into ice, only to reform as it warms up on its 248-year journey!
idea.title: Pluto's Vanishing Atmosphere! 🤯❄️

scenes.visual: A distant, small Pluto with a faint, almost invisible atmospheric haze. Stars twinkle in the dark background.
Close-up of Pluto, showing a very thin, hazy blue layer (atmosphere) around its surface. A small, subtle Earth icon appears briefly next to Pluto for comparison.

scenes.text: Elliptical orbit: 4.4B km to 7.4B km from Sun.
scenes.visual: Animated diagram showing Pluto's highly elliptical orbit around the Sun. Pluto moves along the path, with clear distance markers appearing at its closest and farthest points.

scenes.text: Farthest point: below -240°C. Nitrogen freezes solid.
scenes.visual: Pluto shown at its farthest orbital point. Ice crystals visually form and cover the surface. A thermometer icon drops rapidly to indicate plummeting temperatures.

scenes.text: Atmosphere collapses into ice, then reforms! A 248-year cycle.
scenes.visual: A time-lapse animation of Pluto's atmosphere dramatically shrinking, turning into surface ice, and then gradually reforming as if on a cycle. A subtle clock icon or year counter appears.
